articles.title: Senators approve withholding their own pay during government shutdowns
articles.description:
Lawmakers say the measure would discourage future funding standoffs after a series of record-breaking shutdowns.

The post Senators approve withholding their own pay during government shutdowns appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senators approved a measure to withhold their pay during government shutdowns.
This decision aims to discourage future funding standoffs after record-breaking shutdowns.
Boston residents may see improved government funding stability as a result.

articles.title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter pleads not guilty from hospital bed

summary: Tyler Brown pleads not guilty to shooting two men on Memorial Drive.

articles.title: Inside Jack Schlossberg’s chaotic campaign to revive Camelot
articles.description:

summary: Jack Schlossberg's campaign for a House seat faces high staff turnover and erratic behavior.

Boston residents may see implications for local Democratic strategies and Kennedy legacy discussions.

articles.title: MassINC poll: Healey’s approval down as excitement over T improvements wanes

82% said the state’s transit system is in fair or poor condition.

articles.title: Vance is set to speak in Maine about fraud investigations ahead of primary election
articles.description:

articles.title: Official says World Cup fans should drive to Foxborough and must have ticket
articles.description:
"If you don't have a ticket, don't come to Gillette,” said Jim Nolan, chief operating officer of Kraft Sports & Entertainment.

The post Official says World Cup fans should drive to Foxborough and must have ticket appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jim Nolan advises World Cup fans to drive to Foxborough for matches at Gillette Stadium.

Local traffic may increase significantly on match days, affecting Boston commuters.
